subject: Sociology
title: Cybercrime
description: A micro-lesson that makes Cybercrime usable.

hook.text: A hacker in one country steals from a bank in another while sitting in pajamas. Crime went digital.

buildup.text: Cybercrime includes hacking, identity theft, online fraud, ransomware, and digital harassment at scale.

discovery.text: The internet created crimes without physical presence, borders, or witnesses — challenging traditional policing.

twist.text: Most cybercrime goes unreported and unpunished. Law enforcement lacks the resources and jurisdiction to respond.

climax.text: Cybercrime reveals that legal systems designed for physical space struggle in a borderless digital world.

punchline.text: Crime moved online; the law didn't follow.

quiz.question: What makes cybercrime challenging for law enforcement?
options: It crosses borders and lacks physical evidence
